environment._NOTE_SOCKET: macOS sun_path limit (104 chars): the default bridge socket path under database/daemon-ipc.sock exceeds the limit and the launcher fails with EINVAL. Pin to a short /tmp path so the multi-client bridge can bind.
environment._NOTE_1_DB: Default DB (recommended): context-index__ollama__nomic-embed-text-v1.5__768.sqlite (auto-derived from provider+model+dim). Pull `ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5` for the local-first new-user setup; hf-local with nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is the Python-side fallback.
environment._NOTE_2_TOOLS: Registers 35 tools across families (verified against mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts): memory_save/search/context/match_triggers/index_scan/stats/health/validate/update/delete/bulk_delete/list/drift_why/retention_sweep/get_learning_history/quick_search (16 memory), memory_causal_link/causal_stats (2 causal), memory_ingest_start/status/cancel (3 async ingest), embedder_list/set/status (3 embedder), session_bootstrap/resume/health (3 session), checkpoint_create/list/restore/delete (4 checkpoint), task_preflight/postflight (2 task), eval_run_ablation/reporting_dashboard (2 eval). MCP namespace: mcp__mk_spec_memory__*. Note: coverage-graph and council graph operations are owned by deep-loop-runtime CLI modules and are NOT exposed as MCP tools.
environment._NOTE_CONTEXT_BUDGET: Tool-schema wire payload: ~7,000–8,000 tokens (largest MCP surface; 35 tools across 8 families). Project-wide MCP context budget: ~16–21K tokens total — see code_mode env block for the rollup.
environment._NOTE_3_PROVIDERS: Auto cascade (ADR-014, local-first): Ollama (local) -> hf-local (Python sentence-transformers) -> OpenAI API -> Voyage API.
environment._NOTE_4_EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER: Auto cascade (ADR-014, local-first): Ollama -> hf-local -> OpenAI -> Voyage. Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=<provider> (ollama|hf-local|openai|voyage) to force a specific tier.
environment._NOTE_5_CLOUD_PROVIDERS: Cloud tiers are explicit opt-in: add VOYAGE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY only when you want cloud embeddings; otherwise the cascade keeps everything local. Voyage is embeddings-only here; cloud rerankers (voyage/cohere) were removed in 022/013.
environment._NOTE_8_FEATURE_FLAGS: Opt-OUT flags (default ON, set =false to disable): SPECKIT_ADAPTIVE_FUSION, SPECKIT_SESSION_BOOST, SPECKIT_CAUSAL_BOOST, SPECKIT_HYDE_ACTIVE, SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING, SPECKIT_GRAPH_CONCEPT_ROUTING, SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE=write_local. Opt-IN flags (default OFF): SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER + RERANKER_LOCAL activate the local rerank sidecar (both required post-022/013; no API-key auto-activation). Tier 3 LLM save routing is always on (fail-open to Tier 2 on timeout).
environment._NOTE_9_RERANK_OPT_IN: Rerank sidecar is OPT-IN. To enable: (1) install the system-rerank-sidecar skill, (2) add SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER=true + RERANKER_LOCAL=true to this env block, (3) optionally add RERANK_SIDECAR_PORT=8765 to override the default. Not advertised here by default to keep the boot path minimal.
environment._NOTE_RECOMMENDED_SETUP: New users: install Ollama (https://ollama.com) and run `ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5`. The cascade auto-detects Ollama and persists nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (768d) as the active embedder — no API keys, all-local.

environment._NOTE_1_DB: Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite by default; MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR overrides, with SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR as a legacy fallback (both still recognized by the launcher + handlers).
environment._NOTE_2_TOOLS: Registers 9 tools (8 public + 1 internal trusted-caller): advisor_recommend/rebuild/status/validate plus skill_graph_scan/query/status/validate/propagate_enhances. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_skill_advisor__*
environment._NOTE_3_THRESHOLDS: Advisor confidence/uncertainty thresholds are tunable: SPECKIT_ADVISOR_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (default 0.8), SPECKIT_ADVISOR_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD (default 0.35). Lane weights tunable via SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON + SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_SHADOW_WEIGHTS_JSON (partial-merge JSON, post-022/011/4b).

environment._NOTE_1_DB: Database lives at .opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides. Legacy location (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/) is auto-migrated on first launcher startup.
environment._NOTE_2_TOOLS: Registers 11 tools: code_graph_scan/query/classify_query_intent/context/status/verify/apply, detect_changes, ccc_status/reindex/feedback. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*
environment._NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS: INDEX_* committed defaults are false (end-user safe). Maintainer mode: set SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local (gitignored); the launcher will force all 5 INDEX_* to true at startup. Per-call code_graph_scan args (includeSkills, includeAgents, includeCommands, includeSpecs, includePlugins) still override env for fine-grained control.
environment._NOTE_4_RESCAN_THRESHOLD: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD (default 50) controls when the launcher switches from selective rescan to full scan. Raise to delay full-scans (faster session-boot, more drift tolerance); lower to keep the DB tighter. Post-commit hook (.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/post-commit) also invalidates the SQLite when commits exceed SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD (default 100).

environment._NOTE_2_DEFAULT_EMBEDDER: Default embedder: sbert/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed (sentence-transformers wrapper, 768d, no API key). Promoted over jina-v2-base-code in 018 follow-on after corrected-pipeline bench tied bge-code-v1 on hit rate with lower latency. Source: cocoindex_code/embedders/registered_embedders.py:DEFAULT_EMBEDDER_NAME.
environment._NOTE_3_DEFAULT_RERANKER: Default reranker: Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B (served by the local system-rerank-sidecar). Promoted over jina-v3 in 023B follow-on after 73-probe expanded-fixture bench (n=3, +1 hit/73, -32% p95, Apache-2.0). Reranker is OPT-IN: add SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER=true to activate.
environment._NOTE_CONTEXT_BUDGET: Tool-schema wire payload: ~3–5K tokens (3 tools: search, ccc_search, ccc_status; Python descriptions are verbose).
Tool-schema wire payload: ~1–3K tokens (varies by .utcp_config.json declared tool surface).
environment._NOTE_TOTAL_MCP_BUDGET: Project-wide MCP context-token rollup (rough, wire-payload estimates at ~3.5 chars/token): mk-spec-memory ~7–8K + mk_code_index ~2.2K + mk_skill_advisor ~2.2K + cocoindex_code ~3–5K + sequential_thinking ~0.3K + code_mode ~1–3K = ~16–21K tokens total. On a 200K-context model this is ~8–11% of total budget; on 1M-context Opus ~2%. mk-spec-memory remains the dominant single-MCP cost. Methodology: source-byte ÷ 3.5 × wire-compression factor 0.5; full repro in .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/ (or pipe `tools/list` JSON-RPC to each launcher for exact wire bytes).
